jokes.text: the 83rd or 84th annual e-waste recycling festival!
jokes.setup: Mayor attempting to sound official and ceremonial
jokes.punchline: Admitting uncertainty about which annual festival it is
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective joke about bureaucratic incompetence. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: Sorry. I get aroused in crowds.
jokes.setup: Someone says 'Down in front!' suggesting they're blocking view
jokes.punchline: Reveals inappropriate sexual response to crowds
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and uncomfortable revelation delivered with perfect economy. Classic cringe humor.

jokes.text: this old, inefficient vote rigger!
jokes.setup: Mayor about to throw out ceremonial first item
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to having vote-rigging equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - casually admitting election fraud as if it's normal. Great character integration and subversive edge.

jokes.text: I'll rest easier not knowing where they are.
jokes.setup: Talking about dangerously unstable doomsday devices
jokes.punchline: Preferring ignorance over proper disposal tracking
jokes.explanation: Good ironic logic - the opposite of what responsible leadership would do. Fits the incompetent mayor character well.

jokes.text: Please not to throw away. I give you happy poopie time.
jokes.setup: High-tech Japanese toilet being thrown away
jokes.punchline: Pleading in broken English with childish euphemism
jokes.explanation: Anthropomorphizing technology with innocent desperation. Good character voice and memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: Sorry, you know too much.
jokes.setup: Toilet begging not to be thrown away
jokes.punchline: Mayor treating it like eliminating a witness
jokes.explanation: Perfect noir parody applied to toilet disposal. Great economy and dark humor escalation.

jokes.text: Seems like a good place to ditch some evidence.
jokes.setup: Looking at hazardous waste disposal site
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of evidence tampering
jokes.explanation: Simple dark humor about criminal activity. Functional but not particularly distinctive.

jokes.text: we cause erectile dysfunc... Who are you talking to? No one. Your mama.
jokes.setup: Flexo listing robot problems, gets interrupted
jokes.punchline: Defensive non-sequitur insult when caught talking to himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing robot awkwardness and defensive humor. The 'your mama' is a bit cheap but fits.

jokes.text: by an expendable team of minimum-wage nobodies.
jokes.setup: Explaining how dangerous waste is safely handled
jokes.punchline: Revealing callous disregard for workers
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about how dangerous work is outsourced. Good character consistency for corrupt mayor.

jokes.text: Good news, nobodies!
jokes.setup: Mayor just called workers 'nobodies'
jokes.punchline: Professor cheerfully addressing them as such
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor Farnsworth moment - obliviously cheerful while being insulting. Great callback timing.

jokes.text: to make rat poison and artificial sweeteners.
jokes.setup: Mentioning extracting deadly chromium
jokes.punchline: Using it for both poison and food additives
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark joke about industrial food production. The casual equivalence of rat poison and sweetener is perfect absurdist horror.

jokes.text: This thing is 40% chromium!
jokes.setup: Working on the Planet Express ship
jokes.punchline: Revealing ship's dangerous composition
jokes.explanation: Callback to Bender's '40% [material]' running gag applied to the ship. Functional series humor.

jokes.text: Really? Then don't look over there.
jokes.setup: Someone saying something is the worst they've seen
jokes.punchline: Pointing to something even worse
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation joke setup. Works but is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Let's play find the shiny!
jokes.setup: Children working in toxic conditions
jokes.punchline: Making dangerous work sound like a fun game
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child labor made palatable through innocent language. Good contrast between tone and content.

jokes.text: No, not the whipping.
jokes.setup: Asked if all work is done by children
jokes.punchline: Clarifying that whipping is done by adults
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - seems to deny child labor then reveals something worse. Excellent economy and structure.

jokes.text: Your call to Saint Louis has gone through.
jokes.setup: Leela using an old cell phone
jokes.punchline: Phone still using ancient operator system
jokes.explanation: Good anachronism joke about outdated technology. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: download porno on a crowded bus, even check your e-mail while getting hit by a train.
jokes.setup: Listing eyephone capabilities
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurdly dangerous situations
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane to absurd. The train detail is nicely specific and unexpected.

jokes.text: Well, this is a fine how-do-you-do!
jokes.setup: Everyone abandoning their old electronics
jokes.punchline: Old-fashioned outrage at being discarded
jokes.explanation: Nice anthropomorphization with period-appropriate language

jokes.text: We are on line. It is across town. Stop thinking, fry!
jokes.setup: Confusion between 'online' shopping and waiting in line
jokes.punchline: Bender's frustrated clarification and insult
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'online' vs. 'on line' with characteristic Bender rudeness. Solid character work.

jokes.text: Stop thinking, fry!
jokes.setup: Fry's continued confusion about the line
jokes.punchline: Bender's blunt anti-intellectual advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment with great economy

jokes.text: Is there an app for kissing my shiny metal ass? Several!
jokes.setup: Bender's signature catchphrase as rhetorical question
jokes.punchline: Amy's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Bender's catchphrase with a modern twist. Amy's deadpan delivery makes it work perfectly.

jokes.text: It's that obscure underground song that's constantly playing everywhere.
jokes.setup: Background music playing
jokes.punchline: Contradictory description of 'obscure' yet omnipresent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of hipster music culture and media saturation. Perfect contradiction captures modern music paradox.

jokes.text: Shut up and take my money!
jokes.setup: Salesperson listing product flaws
jokes.punchline: Fry's enthusiastic purchase despite problems
jokes.explanation: One of Futurama's most iconic lines. Perfect consumer culture satire with Fry's impulsive character. Became a widely-used meme.

jokes.text: Say, you're from one of those ethnicities that knows about technology.
jokes.setup: Mom talking to Amy
jokes.punchline: Casually racist assumption delivered politely
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Mom's casual prejudice. The polite delivery makes the racism more uncomfortable and funny.

jokes.text: Screaming during eyephone installation
jokes.setup: Amy explaining she'll install the eyephone
jokes.punchline: Fry screaming in pain during installation
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy. The pain of installation is predictable but fits the show's style.

jokes.text: But don't takey word for it. Unbiased-reviews-dot-mom says
jokes.setup: Recommending product reviews
jokes.punchline: Reviews from obviously biased source (Mom's company)
jokes.explanation: Good satire of fake product reviews and corporate astroturfing. The 'unbiased' name makes the bias more obvious.

jokes.text: And when I say killer app, I mean killer app!
jokes.setup: Mom describing twitcher as a 'killer app'
jokes.punchline: Evil emphasis revealing literal meaning
jokes.explanation: Good double meaning on 'killer app' with Mom's evil character. Simple but effective wordplay.

jokes.text: Enjoy your outdated format, grandpa.
jokes.setup: Professor watching traditional TV
jokes.punchline: Cubert dismissing traditional media
jokes.explanation: Standard generational conflict joke. Good character work for Cubert's smugness but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: They cut off one of my dimensions!
jokes.setup: Professor receiving ransom demand
jokes.punchline: Treating dimensional loss like physical dismemberment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sci-fi absurdism. Taking dimensional kidnapping to its logical extreme. Pure Futurama creativity.

jokes.text: These eyephones are phones, too? Duh.
jokes.setup: Fry surprised eyephones can make calls
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive response to obvious feature
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Fry's obliviousness. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: That's how I can eat this taco. And this spaghetti.
jokes.setup: Leela explaining hands-free flying
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dangerous multitasking while flying
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from simple to absurd multitasking. Sets up the crash that follows.

jokes.text: All I did was add a laugh track and twit it to my 10,000 followers.
jokes.setup: Fry recorded Leela's crash
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to exploiting friend's misfortune
jokes.explanation: Good satire of social media exploitation. Fry's casual tone makes his betrayal more unsettling.

jokes.text: Burping eggs, scratching my underarm fungus, looking for love. Send.
jokes.setup: Fry making hourly social media update
jokes.punchline: Oversharing disgusting personal details
jokes.explanation: Perfect social media oversharing satire with Fry's lack of social awareness. The contrast with 'looking for love' is good.

jokes.text: thanks to twitcher, morons voluntarily spew out every fact I need to exploit them!
jokes.setup: Mom explaining her data collection methods
jokes.punchline: Calling users morons who help their own exploitation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social media satire. Mom's blunt assessment of user behavior is both funny and uncomfortably accurate.

jokes.text: This thing always seems to know when I got the munchies!
jokes.setup: Targeted food advertisement appearing
jokes.punchline: Scruffy amazed by obvious data tracking
jokes.explanation: Good observation about targeted advertising with Scruffy's characteristic obliviousness to technology.

jokes.text: Can I somehow charge it to my eyephone for an additional fee?
jokes.setup: Offered product for his fungus problem
jokes.punchline: Eagerly requests additional charges
jokes.explanation: Good consumer behavior satire showing willingness to pay extra fees

jokes.text: Can you believe 50,000 idiots swallow that crap? Send. Oops.
jokes.setup: Bender lying about solo kayak journey
jokes.punchline: Accidentally sending his contempt for his followers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - contempt for his audience accidentally revealed. Good structure with the 'oops' punchline.

jokes.text: You have 50,000 followers? I only have three. Send. / I unsubscribed yesterday.
jokes.setup: Fry revealing he has very few followers
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately unsubscribing, making it even worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Fry's social media failure with perfect timing

jokes.text: Like this video of you doing karate in your underpants.
jokes.setup: Bender explaining his follower success
jokes.punchline: Humiliating video of Fry
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Bender exploiting his friend. The specificity of 'karate in underpants' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Since when is the Internet about robbing people of their privacy? August 6, 1991.
jokes.setup: Fry's rhetorical question about internet privacy
jokes.punchline: Bender's specific date for when it started
jokes.explanation: Excellent internet history joke. The specific date (close to World Wide Web launch) shows great research and timing.

jokes.text: August 6, 1991.
jokes.setup: When did the Internet become about robbing privacy?
jokes.punchline: Specific early date of web privacy invasion
jokes.explanation: Sharp historical commentary with perfect specificity and economy

jokes.text: What comes out one end, we feed to the other. Also Indian food.
jokes.setup: Asking what they feed the alien goat
jokes.punchline: Disgusting cycle plus casual addition of normal food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant gross-out humor with perfect escalation. The casual addition of 'Indian food' as if it's equivalent is perfect.

jokes.text: Are you dumb bastards listening to me, you dumb bastards?
jokes.setup: Mom addressing her sons
jokes.punchline: Redundant insult showing her contempt
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mom's abusive parenting. The repetition emphasizes her contempt.

jokes.text: for instead of infecting computers, it infects the human brain.
jokes.setup: Describing the twit-worm as a computer virus
jokes.punchline: Revealing it targets human minds instead
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi horror concept with social media implications. The virus metaphor works well for mind control.

jokes.text: The important thing is, we're a family. No, it's not!
jokes.setup: Mom trying to end on positive family note
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicted by her own son
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure showing family dysfunction, great economy

jokes.text: I'm gonna have to swim in the juice of the puke-me-poop-you.
jokes.setup: Fry worried about losing the bet
jokes.punchline: Creative description of alien goat's output
jokes.explanation: Creative gross-out wordplay. The made-up name for the creature is memorable and disgusting.

jokes.text: I'm gonna have to swim in the juice of the puke-me-poop-you.
jokes.setup: Fry realizing he might lose the bet
jokes.punchline: Creative euphemism for the disgusting punishment
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay making the disgusting punishment sound oddly poetic

jokes.text: Who was that... the Queen of England?
jokes.setup: Hearing elegant singing voice
jokes.punchline: Fry's confused attempt to identify refined accent
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Fry's limited cultural knowledge. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: All of its orifices are in use.
jokes.setup: Wondering where singing came from, ruling out the goat
jokes.punchline: Disgusting clarification about creature's state
jokes.explanation: Efficient gross-out humor. The clinical phrasing makes it funnier than explicit description.

jokes.text: I'm not nothing. All my life everyone's called me nothing.
jokes.setup: Leela calling the boil 'nothing'
jokes.punchline: Boil's hurt feelings about being dismissed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - giving a singing boil emotional depth and dignity. Pure Futurama creativity.

jokes.text: A boil kinna have a name?
jokes.setup: Discussion of the boil having a name
jokes.punchline: Boil defending her right to identity
jokes.explanation: Great character development for the boil. The Scottish accent and indignation make it both absurd and touching.

jokes.text: Ew, she's Scottish.
jokes.setup: Susan singing with Scottish accent
jokes.punchline: Fry's prejudiced reaction to nationality
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's casual prejudice. The economy of the line makes it effective.

jokes.text: Million-follower gross.
jokes.setup: Seeing Leela's embarrassing boil situation
jokes.punchline: Measuring grossness by social media potential
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social media satire. Using follower count as a unit of measurement is perfect modern absurdism.

jokes.text: If it ain'ts the world's number ones Internet sensation!
jokes.setup: Leela arriving at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Elzar's excited recognition from viral video
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Elzar's characteristic enthusiasm. Shows the video's impact but isn't particularly creative.

jokes.text: her dignity sac will rupture.
jokes.setup: Worrying about Leela finding out about the video
jokes.punchline: Treating dignity as a physical organ
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Futurama biology absurdism. The concept of a 'dignity sac' is pure creative genius - treating abstract concepts as organs.

jokes.text: Thar, I turned a regular board into a diving board.
jokes.setup: Need for diving board into alien goat fluids
jokes.punchline: Minimal engineering solution presented proudly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hermes' bureaucratic pride in small accomplishments

jokes.text: Sweet Georgia Brown of Kingston Town. It's a tie.
jokes.setup: Announcing bet results
jokes.punchline: Professor's nonsensical exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good Professor Farnsworth random exclamation. The geographic nonsense is characteristic but not exceptional.

jokes.text: between one million and two million zombies.
jokes.setup: Mom excited about having two million zombies
jokes.punchline: Walt's pedantic correction about follower overlap
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Walt's pedantic nature. The math correction deflates Mom's evil excitement nicely.

jokes.text: Like that giant caterpillar what ate Jared from subway.
jokes.setup: Talking about yesterday's news
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to bizarre celebrity death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Futurama absurdism - treating bizarre celebrity death as mundane news. Great dark humor and specificity.

jokes.text: Susan: That's not the only place you need a terrible weight lifted.
jokes.setup: Leela talking about weight lifted from her heart
jokes.punchline: Boil making joke about Leela's weight
jokes.explanation: Good character development - the boil having her own sense of humor and making fat jokes about her host.

jokes.text: Susan: Oh, hold your nose and give it a go.
jokes.setup: Leela refusing another hug due to smell
jokes.punchline: Boil encouraging affection despite circumstances
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing the boil's optimistic personality. The rhyme makes it memorable.
